name: First National Bank of Eastern Arkansas
description_short: First National Bank of Eastern Arkansas is a community bank with 15 branches across Eastern Arkansas, Memphis, and Collierville, offering personal and commercial banking services with over 135 years of history.
description_long: First National Bank of Eastern Arkansas (FNBEA) has operated as an independently owned community bank for over 135 years, establishing itself as the oldest independently owned bank in Eastern Arkansas. The institution maintains its main branch in Forrest City, Arkansas, and operates 15 total branch locations throughout Eastern Arkansas, Memphis, and Collierville, Tennessee. The bank positions itself as a hometown lender focused on personalized service to local citizens, businesses, and farmers in its service region. FNBEA offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services. On the consumer side, they provide personal checking and savings accounts, Visa debit cards, remote deposit capture via smartphone, and mobile banking through their FNBEA Go app. The bank also serves business customers through commercial loan products designed for business growth and development. All deposits are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Their technology platform enables mobile check deposits and online/mobile account management. The bank distinguishes itself through its local ownership structure and extended community history. Unlike many regional banks that have been acquired by larger institutions, FNBEA remains independently owned and operated. Their marketing emphasizes exceptional customer service, personalized attention, and deep roots in Eastern Arkansas communities. The 135-year history is presented as evidence of stability and community commitment. FNBEA appears to be a solid choice for customers seeking traditional community banking with local decision-making and personal service. However, the website provides limited information about competitive product offerings, interest rates, or fee structures. Customers comparing options should evaluate whether the convenience of local branches and personalized service justify any potential rate or fee differences versus larger regional or national banks. The bank's focus on traditional banking suggests it may not offer the advanced digital-only features or aggressive rates that fintech competitors provide.
services: Personal checking accounts with multiple account options
Personal savings accounts with various product tiers
Visa debit cards with cardholder services and benefits
Remote deposit capture using smartphone photo technology
FNBEA Go mobile banking app for account management and mobile deposits
Online banking platform accessible via website login
Commercial loans for business growth and development
Drive-up ATM access at select branch locations
In-person customer service at 15 branch locations
Bill payment services (implied through online banking)
Account enrollment and password reset services
pros: Over 135 years of operating history as an independently owned bank, indicating stability and community roots
15 branch locations across Eastern Arkansas, Memphis, and Collierville for convenient access
FNBEA Go mobile banking app with remote deposit capture via smartphone photo technology
All deposits FDIC-insured with full faith and credit backing of U.S. Government
Visa debit card services with stated benefits and security features
Commercial loan products designed for business growth and development
Personalized customer service approach with dedicated employee staff emphasis
Drive-up ATMs available at main branch and Collierville locations
cons: Website provides no information on interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparing competitiveness
Limited details on commercial loan terms, requirements, or maximum amounts
No mention of online account opening capability; appears to require in-person branch visits
Smaller institution means fewer ATM locations compared to national or regional chains
Website lacks information on credit products, credit cards, or lending rates for personal loans

contact.address: 101 N Washington St, Forrest City, AR 72335

best_for: Small business owners and farmers in Eastern Arkansas seeking relationship-based commercial lending
Residents of Forrest City, Eastern Arkansas, Memphis, and Collierville who value local branch access and personalized service
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits with traditional banking services over digital-only options
Community members seeking to support locally-owned financial institutions
diagnosis: First National Bank of Eastern Arkansas is best for small business owners, farmers, and community members in its service region (Eastern Arkansas, Memphis, Collierville) who prioritize local ownership, relationship banking, and personalized service over aggressive rates or cutting-edge digital features. The main caveat is that the website does not disclose competitive information on interest rates, fees, or loan terms, requiring potential customers to contact branches directly for pricing comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
